name: No Force Unwrapping of Weak References
description: Force-unwrapping a weak variable using the '!' operator triggers a runtime crash if the referenced object has already been deallocated. In a banking application, this leads to unexpected application termination, disrupting user sessions and potentially leaving transactions in an indeterminate state. Always use optional binding (guard let/if let) or optional chaining.

name: Safe Weak Delegate Invocation in Banking Flows
description: Delegates handling sensitive banking operations (such as MFA verification, fund transfers, or account provisioning) are typically declared weak to avoid retain cycles. Force-unwrapping these delegates can cause crashes mid-transaction. If the delegate is nil, gracefully handle the state or log a non-fatal diagnostic event instead of crashing.

name: Test Custom Rule
description: This is a test custom rule description.

name: No Hardcoded API Keys
description: API keys, secrets, and private tokens must never be hardcoded in the codebase. Use dynamic configuration or keychain storage instead.

name: Do Not Cache Sensitive Financial Data in Memory
description: Sensitive banking data such as CVV, PINs, full primary account numbers (PAN), and session tokens must never be stored in long-lived in-memory caches like NSCache or global dictionaries. Storing this data in memory increases the risk of exposure via memory dump attacks or application state snapshots. Use secure enclaves or clear the memory immediately after use.

name: Enforce Limits on NSCache Instances
description: NSCache instances must have explicit limits configured for countLimit and totalCostLimit. Without these limits, the cache can grow unboundedly, consuming excessive system memory and leading to jetsam termination (OOM crashes) by the iOS operating system.

examples.bad:
let statementCache = NSCache<NSString, NSData>()
// No limits set; cache can grow indefinitely

name: Evict Memory Caches on Background and Memory Warnings
description: In-memory caches must be cleared or reduced when the application receives memory warnings or enters the background. This prevents Out-Of-Memory (OOM) crashes and ensures that sensitive cached banking data is not written to disk during iOS background snapshotting.
